chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 147 of 2017 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 08.03.2018 Mr. Muhammad Yakub Kapadia, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner, Mst. Maimoona Dadabhoy has prayed for the grant of Letter of Administration in respect of immoveable property/asset, mentioned in the Schedule of Property attached with the memo of petition, available at page 27, which was left by her deceased husband, namely, Sulleman Dadabhoy s/o. Yusuf Dadabhoy, who died on 15.02.2015 at Karachi, leaving behind Mst. Maimoon Dadabhoy (petitioner/widow), Muhammad Dadabhoy, Nabeel Dadabhoy and Maria Dadabhoy as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased Sulleman Dadabhoy and photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA as well as title documents of the immovable property are also filed at page 21, 25 and 33 to 37. All the legal heirs have executed General Power of Attorney in favour of Shakir Dadabhoy s/o. Ebrahim Dadabhoy, duly attested by the Consulate General of Pakistan at Jeddah, Saudi Arabia and Consular Attache in High Commission of Pakistan at London, United Kingdom, who has filed affidavit of no objection on their behalf for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Asif Hashim Salahjee s/o. Hashim Salahjee and Hussain Muhammad Dadabhoy s/o. Muhammad 2 Hashim Dadabhoy have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record.

Muhammad 2 Hashim Dadabhoy have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 23.05.2017; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 10.08.2017. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Letter of Administration in respect of immovable property/asset shown in the Schedule of Property, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
